guidelines: Use Chivo for all headings to provide an industrial, solid, reliable look suitable for an operations dashboard.
Use IBM Plex Sans for tables, inputs, and dense body content.
Labels and overlines must be heavily tracked uppercase for clear scanning.
Do not use generic fonts like Inter, Roboto, or Open Sans.
Follow 'The Tinted Grey' rule: use Zinc (slightly cool/industrial) instead of pure gray.
Primary CTA uses Deep Blue (Trust/Efficiency), but visual accents and alerts should use Safety Yellow (Road/Driving theme).
Data density requires extreme contrast: Ensure tables use alternating zinc-50/white stripes.

surface_strategy.headers: sticky top-0 z-50 bg-white/80 backdrop-blur-xl border-b border-zinc-200

guidelines: Adopt 'The Control Room' Grid: Density, Data Correlation, Scanability.
Use sharp edges or max 4px radius (rounded-sm) to maintain the functional, technical aesthetic.
Avoid floating 'soft' cards. Use sharp, bordered bento boxes that snap tightly together.
Never center-align dense data. Left-align for fast scanning, right-align numbers/prices.

components.buttons: rounded-none font-bold uppercase tracking-wide text-xs px-6 py-3 transition-colors
components.inputs: rounded-none border-zinc-300 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-600 focus:border-blue-600 bg-white
components.table_headers: bg-zinc-100 text-zinc-500 font-bold uppercase tracking-wider text-xs p-3 border-y border-zinc-200
components.badges: rounded-full px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-wider border
shadcn_overrides: Override default Shadcn rounded corners to `rounded-none` or `rounded-sm`.
Remove heavy drop shadows from dialogs, replace with solid borders (e.g., `border-2 border-zinc-900 shadow-[8px_8px_0_0_rgba(24,24,27,1)]` for a slight brutalist punch, or keep it strictly flat).
Use Shadcn tables but increase data density (tighter padding for cells `p-2` instead of `p-4`).

guidelines: Always explicitly set hover states (e.g., `hover:bg-blue-800 hover:text-white`).
Progressive disclosure: show summary first, reveal 10-class history on click.

image_urls.description: Minimalist Swiss-style poster illustration of a modern motorcycle and scooter. Use as the hero image/background for the admin/trainer login screen.
Abstract top-down view of winding asphalt roads with yellow lines. Use for empty dashboard states or zero-data screens.
Two motorcyclists on a sunny day. Use for trainer profiles or student records.
Man riding a motorcycle down a street next to traffic cones. Perfect for driving school context.
Motorcyclists practicing on a cone course.

icons.style: Regular or Bold weights to match the industrial Swiss font.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install and use Chivo and IBM Plex Sans via Google Fonts.
Use Phosphor Icons for all dashboard icons.
Implement a strict bento-box grid for the dashboard (dense data, minimal whitespace between widgets, clear borders).
Build the 10-class tracker as a visual progress bar with interactive blocks (e.g., 10 small squares, green for completed, red for missed, empty for pending).
For the login page, create a split-screen design: Left side functional login form on solid white, Right side edge-to-edge illustration using the 'login_background' media URL.
Ensure all interactive components have 'data-testid' attributes (e.g., data-testid='mark-attendance-btn').
Do NOT round cards heavily. Stick to `rounded-none` or max `rounded-sm` for an industrial operations feel.
Make the navigation a permanent left-sidebar for Desktop, collapsing to a hamburger menu on Mobile.
Use standard HTML/Tailwind for structural layout and spacing. Use customized Shadcn for complex forms, tables, and dialogs.

accessibility.testing: All elements must have high contrast. Use standard aria-labels for icon-only buttons. Add data-testid to all functional elements.
